name: Salisbury Steaks with Mushroom Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Homemade Beef Broth or commercial low-sodium variety
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine beef, flour, Worcestershire sauce, onion, and spices. Blend gently, thoroughly mixing all ingredients.
2. Add milk and mix again.
3. Shape into eight patties, cover and refrigerate.
4. Meanwhile, place mushrooms in a small bowl. Cover with hot water and soak 20 to 30 minutes; chop and set aside.
5. Strain the water through a paper towel into a clean bowl (this will remove any dirt). Save the liquid.
6. Add enough beef broth to the saved liquid to make 1 cup. Add red wine and stir well.
7. Place a heavy nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add patties and brown them thoroughly on each side. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towels.
8. Pour off any liquid left in skillet and return skillet to heat. Add chopped mushrooms, liquids, and carrots to skillet. Bring liquid to a boil and reduce it by a third.
9. Add beef patties, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes.
10. Sprinkle patties with parsley and serve hot.
11. Serve with baked potatoes and fresh green salad.
